subject: Re: Binning particle stacks instead of average micrographs
body:
Hi Andres,

there have been requests for writing out binned particle stacks in addition 
to unbinned ones during on-the-fly processing to make initial C2D runs less 
IO-intensive. I'll try to get that into the next release or the one after 
that.

I haven't heard of anyone using binning for that purpose. The new mag 
estimation and optics group settings in RELION 3.1 look like they might be 
a better solution for this.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Tuesday, November 12, 2019 at 9:40:10 PM UTC+1, Andres Hernandez wrote:
>
> Hi Dimitry,
> It would be useful to have the option to not bin the average micrograph 
> (just the particle stacks). This way the particles can be re-extracted and 
> re-centered into the unbinned pixel size (useful for large box size 
> workflows, low ram setups, or off-centered particles).
>
> Do you know of anyone using the binned micrographs to match pixel size 
> across scopes? This could be very useful too, I just haven't had data to 
> test it out.
>
